feature: First-party Anthropic API transport (api.anthropic.com)
what: CC builds the Anthropic SDK client and drives messages.create against the first-party endpoint using a resolved API key/auth token; this is the default provider path.
sdkSurface: Options.model/fallbackModel; ANTHROPIC_API_KEY via env/SDK; bundled CLI is the same engine; supportedModels()/accountInfo()
bridge: Provide an API key (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) and the SDK's bundled CLI runs the identical first-party messages.create pipeline.

feature: AWS Bedrock provider transport
what: CC switches to the Bedrock SDK when CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK is set, resolving AWS region/credentials (AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK, AWS_REGION) and Bedrock-specific betas.
sdkSurface: env: CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK (+ AWS_REGION/AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK); passed through Options.env to the bundled CLI
bridge: Set CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK=1 plus AWS credentials in the SDK's `env` (spread process.env first); the CLI selects the Bedrock client.

feature: Google Vertex AI provider transport
what: CC switches to the Vertex SDK when CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX is set, using GCLOUD_PROJECT/CLOUD_ML_REGION and google-auth-library credentials.
sdkSurface: env: CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX (+ GCLOUD_PROJECT/CLOUD_ML_REGION/GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS) via Options.env
bridge: Set CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX=1 and GCP project/region in the SDK `env`; the CLI selects the Vertex client.

feature: Azure AI Foundry provider transport
what: CC switches to the Foundry SDK when CLAUDE_CODE_USE_FOUNDRY is set, using ANTHROPIC_FOUNDRY_API_KEY/_RESOURCE/_BASE_URL and @azure/identity.
sdkSurface: env: CLAUDE_CODE_USE_FOUNDRY (+ ANTHROPIC_FOUNDRY_API_KEY/_RESOURCE/_BASE_URL) via Options.env
bridge: Set CLAUDE_CODE_USE_FOUNDRY=1 plus Foundry resource/key in the SDK `env`; the CLI selects the Foundry client.

feature: Fork-specific OpenAI provider path (src/services/api/openai.ts)
what: The codex-rs fork adds an OpenAI provider/transport (separate from the Anthropic/CLI engine) so the agent can run against OpenAI-family models; this path does not exist in the stock SDK.
ccSource: task brief (fork OpenAI path); not present in spec 22 (Anthropic-only)
bridge: The Agent SDK is the Anthropic/CLI engine only; an OpenAI provider must be built outside the SDK (custom transport or a parallel client). Not reachable via Options.

feature: Custom base URL / gateway / proxy override
what: CC honors ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, CLAUDE_CODE_API_BASE_URL, ANTHROPIC_CUSTOM_HEADERS, and proxy env to point the first-party client at a gateway or LLM proxy.
sdkSurface: Options.env (ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL/ANTHROPIC_CUSTOM_HEADERS/proxy); Options.extraArgs for raw CLI flags
bridge: Set ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL and custom headers through the SDK `env`; the bundled CLI forwards them to the Anthropic client.

feature: Model registry / capability matrix (thinking, fast-mode, structured outputs, 1M context)
what: CC resolves per-model capabilities (modelSupportsThinking/AdaptiveThinking, structured-outputs, cache-editing, max output tokens, Sonnet-1M treatment) and assembles params accordingly.
sdkSurface: Options.model/fallbackModel/thinking/effort/betas(['context-1m-2025-08-07'])/outputFormat; Query.supportedModels()
bridge: The bundled CLI ships the same model registry; set model/thinking/effort/betas/outputFormat and capabilities are applied identically. Query.supportedModels() reads the registry.

feature: Streaming SSE event loop (message_start → content_block_* → message_stop)
what: CC drives the streaming messages.create generator, dispatching SSE part types and accumulating assistant content/usage; partial deltas surface to the UI.
sdkSurface: query() async-iterates SDKMessage; Options.includePartialMessages → SDKPartialAssistantMessage (stream_event)
bridge: The CLI runs the same SSE loop; enable includePartialMessages to receive streaming partials, otherwise consume assistant/result messages.

feature: Retry with exponential backoff + 529 cascade and retry-after handling
what: CC's withRetry retries retryable API errors with jittered exponential backoff (BASE_DELAY_MS=500), honors retry-after, caps consecutive 529s, and drops 529s on non-foreground sources.
sdkSurface: internal to bundled CLI; SDKAPIRetryMessage (system/api_retry) surfaces retries; CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_RETRIES via env
bridge: Retry/backoff is handled inside the CLI engine; observe SDKAPIRetryMessage and tune CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_RETRIES via Options.env. No separate SDK API to override the algorithm.

feature: Streaming → non-streaming fallback on stream errors / 404
what: On a streaming error (or 404 at stream creation), CC falls back to a non-streaming messages.create with MAX_NON_STREAMING_TOKENS cap unless a kill-switch is set.
sdkSurface: internal to bundled CLI; CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONSTREAMING_FALLBACK env to disable
bridge: Fallback is automatic in the CLI engine; disable via CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONSTREAMING_FALLBACK in Options.env if needed.

feature: Normalized API error taxonomy surfaced to the agent
what: classifyAPIError/getAssistantMessageFromError map raw API failures into user-facing categories (prompt_too_long, rate_limit, overload, invalid_api_key, oauth_org_not_allowed, etc.) and assistant error frames.
sdkSurface: SDKResultMessage error subtypes; SDKAssistantMessageError ('rate_limit'|'overloaded'|'authentication_failed'|'oauth_org_not_allowed'|'billing_error'|...)
bridge: The CLI emits the same error classification via SDKResultMessage subtypes and SDKAssistantMessageError; read those discriminants.

feature: Prompt-cache breakpoint placement and 1h TTL eligibility
what: CC inserts ephemeral cache_control markers (addCacheBreakpoints/buildSystemPromptBlocks), gates 1h TTL by subscriber/allowlist, and latches beta headers to keep cache keys stable.
sdkSurface: internal to bundled CLI (no SDK Option toggles individual breakpoints); DISABLE_PROMPT_CACHING env
bridge: Prompt caching is managed by the CLI engine; control coarsely via DISABLE_PROMPT_CACHING* env in Options.env. Breakpoint placement is not exposed.

feature: Token-usage accumulation for cost tracking
what: updateUsage/accumulateUsage preserve cumulative input-side totals across message_start/message_delta and feed cost-tracker; usage flows out per turn.
sdkSurface: SDKResultMessage.usage; Query.getContextUsage(); usage_EXPERIMENTAL_… ; SDKThinkingTokensMessage
bridge: Usage totals arrive on SDKResultMessage and via Query.getContextUsage(); the same accumulation runs in the CLI.

feature: Files API upload/download (session attachments)
what: CC uploads/downloads session files via the Files API with multipart and retry/backoff (BASE_DELAY_MS=500, MAX_RETRIES=3) using the FILES_API_BETA_HEADER.
bridge: No SDK Option exposes the Files API; file attachment plumbing would be built on the Anthropic SDK directly or via custom tooling. Not part of the agent loop surface.

feature: Bootstrap fetch (/api/claude_cli/bootstrap client/model config)
what: CC GETs /api/claude_cli/bootstrap to hydrate clientDataCache and additionalModelOptionsCache at startup.
sdkSurface: internal to bundled CLI startup (no SDK Option); runs automatically with first-party auth
bridge: Bootstrap runs inside the CLI engine when using first-party auth; nothing to wire. (Skipped/irrelevant for Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry.)

feature: Task / budget params (taskBudget, max budget USD)
what: CC assembles output_config task budget and supports a USD budget cap that terminates with a budget-error result.
sdkSurface: Options.taskBudget ({total}); Options.maxBudgetUsd → SDKResultMessage subtype error_max_budget_usd
bridge: Set taskBudget and/or maxBudgetUsd in Options; the CLI assembles the same output_config and emits the budget-error result subtype.

feature: claude.ai (Max/Pro subscription) inference rate limits
what: CC parses claude.ai unified rate-limit headers, surfaces subscription quota/overage messaging, and gates fast-mode/cooldown around claude.ai limits.
bridge: claude.ai subscription rate-limit behavior is tied to claude.ai OAuth login, which Anthropic forbids 3rd-party SDK apps from offering. Use API-key/Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry billing instead; SDKRateLimitEvent exposes API-side limits only.

feature: Generic API rate-limit event surfacing
what: CC surfaces rate-limit info changes to the session so the agent/UI can react to throttling.
sdkSurface: SDKRateLimitEvent (rate_limit_event)
bridge: The CLI emits SDKRateLimitEvent when rate-limit state changes; consume it from the query stream.

feature: MCP server lifecycle (discovery, batched connect, connection cache)
what: CC discovers configured MCP servers, connects in pMap batches (local/remote split), memoizes connections by cache key, and tracks a discriminated connection state per server.
sdkSurface: Options.mcpServers: Record<string,McpServerConfig>; Query.mcpServerStatus() → McpServerStatus[]
bridge: Pass server configs via mcpServers; the bundled CLI runs the same discovery/connect lifecycle and mcpServerStatus() reports per-server state.

feature: MCP stdio transport
what: CC spawns stdio MCP servers via StdioClientTransport (command/args/env, optional CLAUDE_CODE_SHELL_PREFIX, 64MB stderr cap).
sdkSurface: McpStdioServerConfig {type?:'stdio', command, args?, env?, timeout?, alwaysLoad?}
bridge: Configure a stdio entry in mcpServers; the CLI spawns it identically.

feature: MCP SSE transport
what: CC connects SSE MCP servers via SSEClientTransport with auth provider, static+helper headers, and timeout-wrapped fetch.
sdkSurface: McpSSEServerConfig {type:'sse', url, headers?, tools?, timeout?, alwaysLoad?}
bridge: Configure an sse entry in mcpServers with url/headers; the CLI uses the same SSE transport.

feature: MCP Streamable HTTP transport
what: CC connects HTTP MCP servers via StreamableHTTPClientTransport with auth provider and fresh-timeout fetch wrapper.
sdkSurface: McpHttpServerConfig {type:'http', url, headers?, tools?, timeout?, alwaysLoad?}
bridge: Configure an http entry in mcpServers; the CLI uses the same Streamable HTTP transport.

feature: In-process SDK MCP server transport
what: CC supports type:'sdk' servers hosted in-process via SdkControlClientTransport rather than a child process or socket.
sdkSurface: createSdkMcpServer({name,tools}) + tool(); McpSdkServerConfigWithInstance {type:'sdk', name, instance}
bridge: Build the server with createSdkMcpServer/tool and register it under mcpServers with type:'sdk'; the SDK wires the in-process transport.

feature: MCP tool-name prefixing (mcp__server__tool) and no-prefix skip mode
what: CC emits mcp__<server>__<tool> names; the narrow skip-prefix mode is gated by type:'sdk' + CLAUDE_AGENT_SDK_MCP_NO_PREFIX so SDK tools can shadow builtins.
sdkSurface: naming applied by bundled CLI; env CLAUDE_AGENT_SDK_MCP_NO_PREFIX; Options.toolAliases for single-hop redirects
bridge: Default prefixing is automatic; set CLAUDE_AGENT_SDK_MCP_NO_PREFIX (sdk servers) via Options.env, or use toolAliases to redirect builtins to MCP tools.

feature: Automatic reconnect with exponential backoff and session-expiry recovery
what: On remote-server drop, CC reconnects with 1/2/4/8/16s backoff capped 30s (MAX_RECONNECT_ATTEMPTS=5), and recovers from 404+(-32001) session-expired by clearing cache and retrying tool calls once.
sdkSurface: internal to bundled CLI; Query.reconnectMcpServer(serverName) for manual reconnect
bridge: Auto-reconnect/backoff runs inside the CLI; trigger a manual reconnect via Query.reconnectMcpServer.

feature: Manual MCP server reconnect / enable-disable toggle / live set
what: CC exposes reconnectMcpServer and toggleMcpServer plus dynamic set-servers to manage MCP servers mid-session.
sdkSurface: Query.reconnectMcpServer(name); Query.toggleMcpServer(name, enabled); Query.setMcpServers(servers)
bridge: Call the Query control methods on a streaming-input session to reconnect, enable/disable, or replace the MCP server set live.

feature: MCP resources (list/read) exposed as tools
what: CC adds ListMcpResourcesTool/ReadMcpResourceTool for servers advertising resource capability and converts MCP prompts into slash Commands.
sdkSurface: ListMcpResourcesTool/ReadMcpResourceTool (built-in, loaded when servers expose resources); MCP prompts become slash commands
bridge: Resource tools and prompt-commands appear automatically once a configured server advertises them; no extra wiring.

feature: MCP elicitation (form-mode and URL-mode) handling
what: CC registers an elicitation handler, runs elicitation hooks, queues form/url elicitations, and retries tool calls after URL elicitation (-32042) up to 3 times.
sdkSurface: Options.onElicitation (OnElicitation callback); hooks Elicitation/ElicitationResult; SDKElicitationCompleteMessage
bridge: Provide onElicitation (and/or Elicitation hooks) to answer MCP elicitation requests programmatically; the CLI runs the same retry contract. Interactive elicitation UI dialogs → defer to C11.

feature: MCP server OAuth (server auth, step-up, needs-auth cache)
what: CC drives per-server OAuth via ClaudeAuthProvider/performMCPOAuthFlow, wraps fetch with step-up detection, caches needs-auth state (15-min TTL), and surfaces an mcp__server__authenticate pseudo-tool.
sdkSurface: mcpServers headers (static bearer/API key); McpServerStatus.status 'needs-auth'; Options.onElicitation for auth prompts; Query.reconnectMcpServer post-auth
bridge: For pre-obtained tokens, pass Authorization headers in the sse/http mcpServers config. Interactive per-server OAuth flow is driven by the CLI; observe 'needs-auth' status and use onElicitation/reconnect. Full login UI → defer to C11.

feature: Project .mcp.json server approval gate
what: CC presents an approval/multiselect dialog for project-scoped .mcp.json servers and persists approved/rejected lists to local settings.
sdkSurface: Options.strictMcpConfig (only mcpServers, skip filesystem .mcp.json); settingSources controls which settings load; permission rules
bridge: In headless SDK use, pass servers via mcpServers and set strictMcpConfig to bypass the interactive .mcp.json approval gate. The approval DIALOG UI → defer to C11.

feature: Large MCP tool-result truncation / file persistence
what: CC estimates result size and either truncates, persists to a file (returning instructions), or passes through, gated by ENABLE_MCP_LARGE_OUTPUT_FILES.
sdkSurface: internal to bundled CLI; env ENABLE_MCP_LARGE_OUTPUT_FILES via Options.env
bridge: Large-result handling runs in the CLI; toggle ENABLE_MCP_LARGE_OUTPUT_FILES through Options.env.

feature: claude.ai connector MCP proxy (claudeai-proxy transport)
what: CC connects claude.ai connector servers through the claudeai-proxy transport, force-refreshing claude.ai OAuth on 401.
bridge: The claudeai-proxy transport depends on claude.ai OAuth login, which Anthropic forbids for 3rd-party SDK apps. Use stdio/sse/http MCP servers instead; claudeai-proxy is not in the input mcpServers union.

feature: WebSocket and IDE-bridge MCP transports (ws / sse-ide / ws-ide)
what: CC supports ws plus sse-ide/ws-ide transports for IDE integration, including X-Claude-Code-Ide-Authorization headers and an allowed IDE tool list.
bridge: The input McpServerConfig union exposes stdio/sse/http/sdk only — ws and the IDE-bridge transports are not selectable from the SDK. IDE/websocket MCP wiring would be custom.

feature: Strict MCP config (only programmatic servers)
what: CC can restrict the connected set to only the explicitly provided servers, ignoring filesystem .mcp.json/settings.
sdkSurface: Options.strictMcpConfig (boolean, ≈ --strict-mcp-config)
bridge: Set strictMcpConfig:true so only the mcpServers you pass are used.

feature: LSP code intelligence (definitions, references, diagnostics, hover, call hierarchy)
what: CC manages per-language LSP child processes, routes requests by extension, and exposes the LSPTool (gated by ENABLE_LSP_TOOL) for navigation and type errors.
sdkSurface: LSP built-in tool (loaded via tools-reference); requires a code-intelligence plugin supplying server config
bridge: Provide an LSP server via a plugin (plugins Option) and enable the LSP tool; the bundled CLI runs the same manager. Server config is plugin-only.

feature: Plugin-supplied LSP server configuration (no user/project setting path)
what: LSP servers are configured exclusively through plugins (ScopedLspServerConfig: command/args/env/extensionToLanguage/...); there is no settings.json path.
sdkSurface: Options.plugins: SdkPluginConfig[] (type:'local', path) loads the plugin that ships the LSP server config
bridge: Bundle the LSP server config in a local plugin and load it via the plugins Option; this is the only supported configuration channel, matching CC.

feature: Passive diagnostics pipeline (publishDiagnostics → attachment, dedup, volume caps)
what: CC captures textDocument/publishDiagnostics passively, dedups across batches/turns, caps per-file (10) and total (30), and delivers diagnostics as attachments on the next turn.
sdkSurface: internal to bundled CLI LSP manager; surfaces as attachments/system context when LSP is active
bridge: The diagnostic capture/dedup/delivery pipeline runs inside the CLI whenever an LSP server is loaded; no separate SDK API.

feature: LSP server lifecycle: crash recovery, restart, content-modified retry
what: CC tracks per-server state, restarts on crash up to maxRestarts (default 3), and retries ContentModified (-32801) requests with 500/1000/2000ms backoff.
sdkSurface: internal to bundled CLI LSP manager (no SDK Option for restart/backoff tuning)
bridge: Lifecycle, crash recovery, and request retry are handled by the CLI's LSP manager; behavior matches CC with no SDK overrides.

feature: File-sync notifications on write/edit (didOpen/didChange/didSave) and diagnostic clearing
what: FileWrite/FileEdit tools call saveFile and clearDeliveredDiagnosticsForFile so the LSP server re-analyzes edited files and re-emitted diagnostics are not suppressed.
sdkSurface: automatic when LSP active and built-in Edit/Write tools run; no SDK-specific API
bridge: File tools fire LSP sync automatically in the CLI when an LSP server is loaded; nothing to wire on the SDK side.

feature: Bare/headless short-circuit of LSP
what: CC skips LSP initialization in bare/SIMPLE mode since scripted -p calls have no editor-integration use for it.
sdkSurface: behavior of bundled CLI; LSP simply absent unless a plugin supplies a server and the tool is enabled
bridge: In headless SDK runs LSP is inactive by default (no plugin/server), matching CC's bare-mode short-circuit; enable explicitly via a plugin when wanted.

feature: claude.ai (Max/Pro) OAuth login flow with PKCE
what: CC runs the Authorization Code + PKCE flow against claude.ai/claude.com, spins a localhost callback listener, and stores OAuthTokens to log a subscription user in.
bridge: Anthropic contractually forbids 3rd-party SDK apps from offering claude.ai OAuth login. Use API key / Bedrock / Vertex / Foundry auth instead.

feature: Console (platform.claude.com) OAuth + API-key creation flow
what: CC supports the Console OAuth path (org:create_api_key scope) and createAndStoreApiKey to mint and persist a first-party API key.
bridge: The interactive Console OAuth login is part of the forbidden claude.ai/Console login surface for 3rd-party SDK apps. Provide an already-issued ANTHROPIC_API_KEY instead.

feature: API key authentication (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / apiKeyHelper)
what: CC resolves a first-party API key from env, file descriptor, apiKeyHelper script, or keychain with a defined priority order.
sdkSurface: env: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY via Options.env; apiKeyHelper via settings; bundled CLI resolves identically
bridge: Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in Options.env (or configure apiKeyHelper in settings); this is the primary supported SDK auth path.

feature: Long-lived OAuth token via CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN env
what: CC accepts a pre-issued inference-only OAuth token (scopes ['user:inference'], refreshToken/expiresAt null) from CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN or a file descriptor.
sdkSurface: env: CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN via Options.env (inference-only token)
bridge: Pass a pre-minted CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN through Options.env; the CLI uses it as an inference-only bearer (no interactive login, no refresh).

feature: Refresh-token rotation with cross-process lockfile
what: CC reactively refreshes OAuth tokens (grant_type=refresh_token) with a 5-min expiry buffer under a filesystem lock so concurrent CLIs don't race.
bridge: Refresh rotation is intrinsic to the claude.ai/Console OAuth login that 3rd-party SDK apps may not offer. API keys do not expire; for expiring tokens the host app must re-issue them out-of-band.

feature: 401 recovery via token refresh / keychain re-read
what: CC's handleOAuth401Error dedups concurrent recoveries, re-reads tokens (another tab may have refreshed), and forces a refresh on a stale access token.
bridge: This recovery path is specific to claude.ai OAuth tokens, which the SDK cannot use. API-key auth has no 401-refresh cycle; auth failures surface as SDKAssistantMessageError 'authentication_failed'.

feature: macOS Keychain credential storage (security(1) read/write/delete + cache)
what: CC stores OAuthTokens/trusted-device tokens in the macOS Keychain via the security(1) subprocess with a 30s in-process cache, generation guard, and mtime invalidation.
bridge: OS-keychain credential storage is part of CC's interactive login flow; the SDK takes auth via env/settings. A host app wanting keychain storage manages it itself and injects the resolved key into Options.env.

feature: Plain-text credential fallback (.credentials.json, mode 0600)
what: On non-darwin (or keychain failure) CC stores credentials in ~/.claude/.credentials.json with 0600 perms and a plaintext warning.
bridge: Token-file storage backs the interactive login the SDK cannot perform; host apps that persist credentials manage their own storage and pass the key via env.

feature: Trusted-device enrollment (X-Trusted-Device-Token)
what: CC enrolls a trusted device (POST /api/auth/trusted_devices) shortly after login behind a GrowthBook gate and attaches X-Trusted-Device-Token to bridge calls.
bridge: Trusted-device enrollment is bound to claude.ai OAuth login and the claude.ai session/bridge surface, neither available to 3rd-party SDK apps.

feature: Third-party provider auth disables the 1P OAuth path
what: isAnthropicAuthEnabled() turns off the first-party OAuth path when Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry are active, deferring entirely to cloud-provider credentials.
sdkSurface: env: CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK/_USE_VERTEX/_USE_FOUNDRY via Options.env
bridge: Setting a provider env var routes auth entirely to the cloud provider's credentials, exactly as in CC; no Anthropic OAuth is attempted.

feature: Account info / subscription type / profile scope lookups
what: CC exposes getOauthAccountInfo/getSubscriptionType/isClaudeAISubscriber/hasProfileScope derived from the OAuth profile and account UUIDs.
sdkSurface: Query.accountInfo() → AccountInfo
bridge: Query.accountInfo() returns the active account/auth info. Subscription-tier fields that depend on claude.ai OAuth are not populated under API-key auth.

feature: Custom approved OAuth base URL override (FedStart/staging allow-list)
what: CLAUDE_CODE_CUSTOM_OAUTH_URL rewrites all OAuth endpoints to an approved base (FedStart/staging) or throws if not allow-listed.
bridge: This only retargets the (forbidden) claude.ai/Console OAuth login to approved gov/staging hosts; with no OAuth login in the SDK there is nothing to retarget. Use a gateway base URL for API-key traffic instead (see 22.6).
